openmeter/productcatalog/planaddon/adapt.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing planaddon.Repository — the persistence layer for plan-to-addon assignment records. Owns all SQL, soft-delete logic, and eager-loading of related plan and addon entities via the Tx/WithTx/Self triad required by entutils.TransactingRepo.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping every method
patterns.description: Every exported method wraps its body in entutils.TransactingRepo[T, *adapter](ctx, a, fn). The fn closure receives a tx-rebound *adapter; never call a.db outside the closure.

patterns.name: Tx/WithTx/Self triad
patterns.description: adapter implements Tx (a.db.HijackTx + entutils.NewTxDriver), WithTx (entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig), and Self (returns a). All three are required for TransactingRepo to rebind.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txClient := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txClient.Client(), logger: a.logger} }

patterns.name: Post-create/update refetch with eager loads
patterns.description: After Save()/Update(), re-query with WithPlan(PlanEagerLoadPhasesWithRateCardsWithFeaturesFn).WithAddon(AddonEagerLoadRateCardsWithFeaturesFn) because Ent Save() does not populate edges.

patterns.name: Soft-delete via SetDeletedAt + clock.Now()
patterns.description: Delete sets DeletedAt to clock.Now().UTC() via UpdateOneID, never hard-deletes. List/Get add planaddondb.DeletedAtIsNil() unless IncludeDeleted is set.

patterns.name: Typed NotFoundError wrapping
patterns.description: Catch entdb.IsNotFound(err) and re-wrap as planaddon.NewNotFoundError(NotFoundErrorParams{...}) so callers get a domain-typed error.
patterns.example: if entdb.IsNotFound(err) { return nil, planaddon.NewNotFoundError(planaddon.NotFoundErrorParams{Namespace: params.Namespace, ID: params.ID}) }

patterns.name: Shared package-level eager-load vars
patterns.description: PlanEagerLoadPhasesWithRateCardsWithFeaturesFn and AddonEagerLoadRateCardsWithFeaturesFn are reused across queries to keep edge loading consistent.

patterns.name: Validated Config constructor
patterns.description: New(Config) validates required fields (Client, Logger) via Config.Validate() and errors rather than panics. var _ models.Validator = (*Config)(nil) enforces the interface.

key_file_guides.role: Config, New constructor, adapter struct with Tx/WithTx/Self. No query logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must implement the full TxCreator+TxUser triad exactly or TransactingRepo fails to rebind. var _ planaddon.Repository = (*adapter)(nil) must pass.

key_file_guides.role: All CRUD method implementations; each closure passed to entutils.TransactingRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call a.db outside a TransactingRepo closure. DeletePlanAddon must keep the get-then-soft-delete pattern.

key_file_guides.role: FromPlanAddonRow converts entdb.PlanAddon to planaddon.PlanAddon, delegating edge mapping to planadapter.FromPlanRow and addonadapter.FromAddonRow.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Edges are optional — guard with if a.Edges.Plan != nil / if a.Edges.Addon != nil before dereferencing.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests against real Postgres via pctestutils.NewTestEnv, covering Create/Get/List/Update/Delete.
key_file_guides.watch_for: env.DBSchemaMigrate(t) must run before any query. Tests use context.Background() (pre-t.Context() style) — keep consistent.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db directly without wrapping in entutils.TransactingRepo — bypasses ctx-bound transaction.
Hard-deleting rows via Delete() instead of SetDeletedAt soft-delete.
Returning raw entdb.IsNotFound errors instead of planaddon.NewNotFoundError.
Adding business-logic validation (plan status, conflict detection) in the adapter — belongs in the service.
Skipping eager-load vars when re-fetching after create/update — callers expect populated Plan/Addon edges.

decisions.rationale: Ent transactions propagate via ctx; the raw *entdb.Client falls off a caller transaction and causes partial writes under concurrency.

decisions.decision: Post-create refetch instead of Save() return value.
decisions.rationale: Ent's Save() does not populate relation edges; a query with WithPlan/WithAddon is the only way to return a hydrated object.
Assignments must stay queryable (with IncludeDeleted) for audit and reconciliation after removal.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new mutation method following the adapter pattern

openmeter/productcatalog/planaddon/httpd.purpose: HTTP handler layer for plan-addon assignment CRUD endpoints, bridging the v1 REST API (api.PlanAddon*) to planaddon.Service via httptransport.HandlerWithArgs. Owns request decoding, response mapping, and error encoding only — no business logic.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs per endpoint
patterns.description: Each handler method returns a typed HandlerWithArgs[Request, Response, Params] built inline with decoder, operation, and ResponseEncoder closures. Never implement ServeHTTP directly.

patterns.name: Namespace via h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
patterns.description: All decoders call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) delegating to h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx). Never read namespace from URL params or headers directly.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: ValidationErrorEncoder per operation
patterns.description: Every operation appends httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ValidationErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ResourceKindAddon)) so domain validation errors render as RFC 7807.

patterns.name: Local type aliases in planaddon.go
patterns.description: Each endpoint declares local type aliases (e.g. CreatePlanAddonRequest = planaddon.CreatePlanAddonInput) next to its handler type for self-documentation.
patterns.example: type (CreatePlanAddonRequest = planaddon.CreatePlanAddonInput; CreatePlanAddonResponse = api.PlanAddon; CreatePlanAddonHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...])

patterns.name: Handler interface embeds sub-interfaces
patterns.description: Handler embeds PlanAddonHandler, which declares one factory method per endpoint. var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) enforces compile-time satisfaction.
patterns.example: type Handler interface { PlanAddonHandler }; type PlanAddonHandler interface { ListPlanAddons() ListPlanAddonsHandler; ... }

key_file_guides.role: Handler and PlanAddonHandler interfaces, handler struct, New constructor, resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New endpoints must appear in both PlanAddonHandler and the struct for var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil). resolveNamespace returns 500 (not 400) on missing namespace.

key_file_guides.role: One function per HTTP operation, each returning a HandlerWithArgs with inline decoder/operation closures.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Status codes must match the spec: 200 list/get/update, 201 create, 204 (EmptyResponseEncoder) delete. No business logic here.

key_file_guides.role: FromPlanAddon converts domain to api.PlanAddon; AsCreatePlanAddonRequest/AsUpdatePlanAddonRequest convert API bodies to domain inputs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FromPlanAddon calls a.AsProductCatalogPlanAddon().ValidationErrors() — propagate validation issues into the response ValidationErrors field, never swallow.

anti_patterns: Implementing net/http.Handler directly — always use httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs.
Resolving namespace from URL path params instead of h.resolveNamespace(ctx).
Omitting WithErrorEncoder(ValidationErrorEncoder(...)) — validation errors will not serialize correctly.
Adding business logic (status checks, conflict detection) in decoder or mapping — decoders parse only.
Returning raw domain errors without letting the error encoder chain handle them.

decisions.rationale: Separates HTTP concerns from logic and enables consistent middleware (OTel, error encoding) without boilerplate.

decisions.decision: Namespace injected via NamespaceDecoder, not path/query param.
decisions.rationale: Self-hosted uses a static default namespace; the decoder abstraction supports both static and multi-tenant resolvers without changing handlers.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new read-only endpoint following the httpdriver pattern

openmeter/productcatalog/planaddon/servi.purpose: Business-logic layer implementing planaddon.Service — validates inputs, enforces plan/addon state constraints (status, deleted checks), delegates persistence to planaddon.Repository, and publishes domain events after every mutation. No Ent or SQL imports allowed.

patterns.name: transaction.Run / RunWithNoValue for writes
patterns.description: Mutating methods (CreatePlanAddon, DeletePlanAddon, UpdatePlanAddon) wrap their fn in transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, fn) for atomicity across adapter calls and event publishing.

patterns.name: Validate params first, then cross-entity checks
patterns.description: Each method calls params.Validate() on entry, then fetches related entities (plan, addon) to enforce status/deleted rules before calling the adapter.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid ...: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Domain errors wrapped as models.Generic*
patterns.description: plan/addon/planaddon NotFoundError caught via errors.As and re-wrapped as models.NewGenericNotFoundError; validation -> NewGenericValidationError; duplicates -> NewGenericConflictError.
patterns.example: if notFound := &(plan.NotFoundError{}); errors.As(err, &notFound) { return nil, models.NewGenericNotFoundError(err) }

patterns.name: Publish domain event after every mutation
patterns.description: After successful create/update/delete, publish a typed event (NewPlanAddonCreateEvent/UpdateEvent/DeleteEvent) via s.publisher inside the transaction closure — publish failure rolls back the DB change.
patterns.example: event := planaddon.NewPlanAddonCreateEvent(ctx, planAddon); if err = s.publisher.Publish(ctx, event); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Config nil-checks in New()
patterns.description: service.New(Config) checks Adapter, Plan, Addon, Logger, Publisher for nil and errors, enforcing complete wiring. var _ planaddon.Service = (*service)(nil) enforces the interface.
patterns.example: if config.Adapter == nil { return nil, errors.New("add-on assignment adapter is required") }

patterns.name: Patch-field merge mirrored with adapter
patterns.description: UpdatePlanAddon builds a merged draft PlanAddon for Validate() before calling the adapter. Merge rules (MaxQuantity always via SetOrClearMaxQuantity; FromPlanPhase/Metadata/Annotations only when non-nil) must match the adapter exactly.
patterns.example: // MaxQuantity is always replaced via SetOrClearMaxQuantity, so nil clears the column.

key_file_guides.role: Config, New constructor, service struct. No method logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: var _ planaddon.Service = (*service)(nil) compile-check — add interface methods here before implementing in planaddon.go.

key_file_guides.role: All service method implementations. Reads call fn(ctx) directly; writes use transaction.Run / RunWithNoValue.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DeletePlanAddon re-fetches after deletion for the DeletedAt timestamp in the event payload — do not skip. Always publish inside the transaction closure.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests of service+adapter+DB via pctestutils.NewTestEnv, covering invalid-state transitions (published plan rejects new plan-addon).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests create meters/features/plans/addons as prerequisites — reuse pctestutils helpers, don't duplicate fixtures.

anti_patterns: Calling s.adapter outside a transaction.Run closure for writes — risks partial writes if publisher.Publish fails.
Returning adapter-level errors (planaddon.NotFoundError) without wrapping in models.Generic* — breaks the HTTP error encoder.
Checking plan/addon status inside the adapter — status validation belongs in the service.
Omitting event publishing after a successful mutation — downstream systems depend on these events.
Using context.Background() instead of the passed ctx — breaks tracing and cancellation.

decisions.rationale: Create/Update/Delete each do get-then-mutate and publish an event; all must be atomic so a publish failure rolls back the DB change.

decisions.decision: Cross-entity validation in the service, not the adapter.
decisions.rationale: Adapters are pure persistence; rules about which states allow assignment must be in the service to stay testable without a DB.

decisions.decision: Patch-field merge mirrored between service and adapter.
decisions.rationale: UpdatePlanAddon builds a draft merged state for Validate() that must match the adapter's SetOrClear/conditional-set behaviour to avoid silent divergence.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new write method with transaction, validation, event publishing
